title: 4 Ways to Reduce Microplastics in Your Body

chunks.title: Microplastics and Their Impact
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with the prevalence of microplastics in the human body and the environment. Various sources of these microplastics are identified, especially focusing on plastics used for packaging and containers that come into contact with food.
chunks.transcript: Microlastics are also a big conversation right now. This is something that you've spoken out about quite a bit. How are we exposed to different micro microlastics in our environment and what are the negative impacts of them? >> So, if you look I mean you've seen the studies now. They look in human testicles, human ovaries, human brains. There's microplastics everywhere. That's scary to think my brain has an appreciable amount of microplastics in it. >> And these are essentially like petroleum byproducts, right? >> Yep. small fragments of plastic. So, unfortunately, we don't we absorb them and we hold on to some of them and our body probably detoxifies them gradually in sort of a flux um as a flux, you know, in and out. But I think the most important thing with microplastics is to understand like the biggest sources of exposure. When I've looked at sheer numbers, I think the biggest source of exposure is plastics, like plastic water bottles, plastic lined containers. But there are a lot of those things that I think a lot that many of us don't have an awareness of. So obviously getting your bottled water in plastic is a horrible idea. Thousands, tens of thousands, hundreds of thousands of microplastics per liter, especially if it's been sitting on the shelf at Costco for 6 months or sitting in your car in 200°ree heat as the car is turning into a sauna. Horrible idea. But all cans are lined with plastic. So, soda cans, the worst can is a soup can because soup cans are filled with hot soup. >> Yeah. >> So, the thing you don't want to do is to put hot liquids into plastic. And believe me, when I was in PA school, I had plastic containers and I took my chicken and pasta sauce and I put it in the microwave and I do myself with microplastics just like the rest of us, right? Oh, this is great. I didn't even think about it. Just so unaware. But you do not want to put hot things into plastic. That's a horrible idea. So soups, all these things really, that's probably one of the biggest exposures that we're not aware of. I was pretty I was pretty surprised by the numbers with tea and tea bags. They're like the pyramidal shaped tea >> and they're like kind of pearly looking. They look really pretty, but yeah, I see those a lot at spas. And I used to drink them and now I I just I just imagine all of the plastic that I'm drinking when I drink them and I can't do it anymore. >> Well, the worst one for me is um do you ever have a camel back? Yeah. >> And drinking out of plastic bottles when I ride my road bike or my mountain bike. >> And you just don't know with those bottles. Like if they've been sitting in a hot warehouse for however long, water doesn't expire. I mean, fish is another big exposure of microplastics, too, because they're in the ocean. They're swimming in it literally, and they're bioaccumulating this. So, what do we do? So look, none of us is going to be free of microplastics. One of the most challenging things is just getting meat, you know. chunks.title: Microplastics in Food and Beverages
chunks.summary: The conversation expands to microplastics found in food and beverages, particularly highlighting dangers associated with plastic-lined cans and containers. The speaker emphasizes the importance of being conscious about food packaging and its health implications.

chunks.title: Challenges with Meat Packaging
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to the challenges of purchasing meat that is often packaged in plastic. The host explores alternatives and practices to minimize exposure to microplastics from plastic containers while handling meat products.

chunks.title: Coffee Preparation and Plastic Exposure
chunks.summary: The segment addresses the concerns related to coffee preparation methods using plastic and aluminum components. The speaker discusses how common conveniences can lead to increased exposure to harmful substances due to plastic interactions when hot water is involved.

chunks.title: Plastics in Clothing and Health Implications
chunks.summary: The conversation concludes with a focus on microplastics and synthetic materials in clothing, particularly polyester. The hosts share studies highlighting potential health issues related to wearing synthetic fibers, especially concerning male fertility.
